title: senderExpiresAt stored raw without SQLite datetime normalization
description: In jmap.rs message_set, the senderExpiresAt field from client JSON is stored directly into expires_at without format normalization. If the client sends an RFC 3339 string with a 'T' separator (e.g. '2026-04-24T12:00:00Z'), the hard_delete_expired_messages() comparison 'expires_at <= datetime("now")' will silently fail — SQLite's datetime() returns 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' format and lexicographic comparison against an RFC 3339 string with 'T' gives incorrect results. Messages will not be deleted at the intended time (or may never be deleted). Fix: normalize expires_at to 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' format before insertion, matching the sentinel used in hard_delete_expired_messages.
